markets.title: Bitcoin above $100k by year-end 2026
markets.subtitle: Will BTC/USD exceed $100,000 before January 1, 2027?
markets.rules_primary: This market will resolve to Yes if the price of Bitcoin (BTC) in US dollars exceeds $100,000.00 at any point before 11:59:59 PM ET on December 31, 2026, as reported by CoinGecko. Otherwise, this market will resolve to No.
markets.rules_secondary: The resolution source is CoinGecko (coingecko.com). In the event CoinGecko is unavailable, CoinMarketCap will be used as a backup source.

markets.title: Fed rate cut March 2026
markets.subtitle: Will the FOMC reduce the federal funds rate at the March 2026 meeting?
markets.rules_primary: This market will resolve to Yes if the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) reduces the target range for the federal funds rate at the conclusion of its scheduled meeting on March 17-18, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to No.
markets.rules_secondary: Resolution will be based on the official FOMC statement released at the conclusion of the meeting, as published on federalreserve.gov.

markets.title: S&P 500 above 6,000 on March 31, 2026
markets.subtitle: Will the S&P 500 close above 6,000 on March 31, 2026?
markets.rules_primary: This market will resolve to Yes if the closing price of the S&P 500 index (SPX) on March 31, 2026 is strictly above 6,000.00. If the market is closed on March 31, the most recent prior closing price will be used. Otherwise, this market will resolve to No.
markets.rules_secondary: The resolution source is the official S&P 500 closing price as reported by S&P Dow Jones Indices.

markets.title: US government shutdown before April 2026
markets.subtitle: Will there be a US federal government shutdown before April 1, 2026?
markets.rules_primary: This market will resolve to Yes if a partial or full US federal government shutdown occurs at any point before 12:00:00 AM ET on April 1, 2026. A shutdown is defined as a lapse in federal appropriations resulting in the furlough of non-essential federal employees. Otherwise, this market will resolve to No.
markets.rules_secondary: Resolution will be based on official reporting from the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) and major news sources (AP, Reuters).

markets.title: Ethereum above $5,000 by end of June 2026
markets.subtitle: Will ETH/USD reach $5,000 before July 1, 2026?
markets.rules_primary: This market will resolve to Yes if the price of Ethereum (ETH) in US dollars reaches or exceeds $5,000.00 at any point before 11:59:59 PM ET on June 30, 2026, as reported by CoinGecko. Otherwise, this market will resolve to No.
markets.rules_secondary: The resolution source is CoinGecko (coingecko.com). In the event CoinGecko is unavailable, CoinMarketCap will be used as a backup source.

markets.title: Trump approval rating above 50% in March 2026
markets.subtitle: Will President Trump's approval rating exceed 50% in any major poll in March 2026?
markets.rules_primary: This market will resolve to Yes if President Donald Trump's approval rating exceeds 50% in any poll conducted by Gallup, Pew Research, or the RealClearPolitics average during the month of March 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to No.
markets.rules_secondary: Resolution will be based on publicly available poll results from the specified sources.

markets.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5 before July 2026
markets.subtitle: Will OpenAI publicly release a model officially named GPT-5 before July 1, 2026?
markets.rules_primary: This market will resolve to Yes if OpenAI publicly releases (makes available for general use, including via API) a model officially designated as 'GPT-5' before 12:00:00 AM ET on July 1, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to No.
markets.rules_secondary: Resolution will be based on official OpenAI announcements and blog posts at openai.com.

markets.title: Super Bowl LXI winner: Kansas City Chiefs
markets.subtitle: Will the Kansas City Chiefs win Super Bowl LXI?
markets.rules_primary: This market will resolve to Yes if the Kansas City Chiefs win Super Bowl LXI. Otherwise, this market will resolve to No.
markets.rules_secondary: Resolution will be based on the official NFL game result.
